channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Tech workers in Kenya hold vigil for TikTok content moderator who died
item.description: Technology workers in Kenya have held a vigil for a colleague who died in unclear circumstances after she was unable to travel to her home in Nigeria for two years.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Gov tech layoffs could hamper SHARE IT Act software mandates
item.description: The SHARE IT Act requires agencies to share custom-developed software code. But the termination of 18F and other tech experts could hamper the law's goals. The post Gov tech layoffs could hamper SHARE IT Act software mandates first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: New CEO Appointed at Intel: Lip-Bu Tan Takes Helm Amid Chaotic Situation
item.description: Intel announces new CEO Lip-Bu Tan, a semiconductor industry veteran with experience in software and electronics design automation, replacing interim co-CEOs David Zinsner and Michelle Johnston Holthaus.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Apple successfully blocks Match from data in India antitrust case
item.description: Apple has successfully blocked its opponents in India, Tinder-owner Match and a group of startups, from accessing its commercially sensitive information which was part of antitrust findings against the U.S. firm, a confidential order shows.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Arista delivers intelligent load balancing, AI job-centric observability
item.description: Arista Networks has added load balancing and AI job-centric observability features to core software products in an effort to help enterprise customers grow and effectively manage AI networked environments.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: FTC asks judge to delay Amazon trial due to resource constraints
item.description: HALELUYA HADERO The Federal Trade Commission asked a federal judge on Wednesday to delay a trial in a case accusing Amazon of using deceptive practices in its Prime subscription program, citing staffing and budgetary challenges at the government agency.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: OpenAI Unveils Responses API and Upgraded Agents SDK
item.description: OpenAI's new tools aim to make it easier for enterprises to build agents with advanced reasoning and multimodal capabilities, potentially countering challenges from rivals like Anthropic and Chinese competitors.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How the Trump administration plans to use algorithms to target protesters
item.description: As protests against the Trump administration and in favor of Palestine continue to grow across the country, the U.S. State Department is reportedly planning to use tech to try and tamp down on dissent.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: 6 in 10 say Musk lacks experience, judgment to change government: Survey
item.description: Elon Musk, a key political ally of President Trump, is viewed negatively by most Americans and they question his qualifications to overhaul the federal government, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: Google debuts AI model for robotics, challenging Meta, OpenAI
item.description: Research unit Google DeepMind will release Gemini Robotics, a new branch of its flagship AI model aimed at developing robots.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: New tools from OpenAI help companies create their own AI agents
item.description: OpenAI on Tuesday launched Responses, a new api intended to eventually replace Assistants. The big draw? Responses provides a number of new tools that companies and organizations can use to create their own AI agents.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: French publishers and authors sue Meta over copyright works used in AI training
item.description: French publishers and authors said Wednesday they're taking Meta to court, accusing the social media company of using their works without permission to train its artificial intelligence model.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Multicloud: Tips for getting it right
item.description: More and more companies are adopting a multicloud strategy. The goal? To maximize the flexibility and performance of their IT landscapes. They combine cloud services from different providers in a targeted way to exploit the advantages of different platforms.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Elon Musk’s White House role weighs on business empire
item.description: Elon Musk’s role in the Trump administration is complicating his business ventures, as the tech billionaire pushes forward with efforts to slash the federal government and budget in the face of mounting criticism.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Altera targets low-latency AI edge applications with new FPGA products
item.description: Altera has introduced the latest family of Agilex FPGAs, along with Quartus Prime Pro software, and FPGA AI Suite to enable the rapid development of highly customized embedded systems for use in robotics, factory automation systems, and medical equipment.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Nebraska Legislature approves measure classifying gig economy workers as independent contractors
item.description: The Nebraska Legislature has approved a measure classifying some gig economy workers as independent contractors, according to its website. L.B. 229, introduced by state Sen. Bob Hallstrom, was approved Monday, with 33 voting in favor and 15 opposed.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Alphabet’s Waymo to offer self-driving rides in Silicon Valley
item.description: The company will offer rides to a select group of residents before expanding to more riders in a 27-square-mile swath of the South Bay that includes Mountain View, Los Altos, Palo Alto and parts of Sunnyvale.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Hey Apple! When you’re in a hole, stop digging
item.description: Soon after the non-appearance of the most important Apple Intelligence feature, Apple now finds itself in danger of digging an even deeper hole by promising iOS 19 will be the most significant upgrade in years. The problem: it again runs the risk of promising too much and not delivering enough.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: DOGE deploys AI chatbot at GSA amid workforce reductions
item.description: The Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), backed by Elon Musk, has expanded the deployment of its custom AI-powered chatbot, GSAi, to approximately 1,500 government workers at the General Services Administration.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Word for Microsoft 365 cheat sheet
item.description: Word has always been the workhorse app of the Microsoft Office suite. Nearly everyone who uses Office ends up using Word at some point, whether it be for writing memos, typing up agendas, creating reports, crafting business correspondence or any of a thousand other uses.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Elon Musk claims X being targeted in 'massive cyberattack' as service goes down
item.description: Hours after a series of outages Monday that left X unavailable to thousands of users, Elon Musk claimed that the social media platform was being targeted in a “massive cyberattack.” “We get attacked every day, but this was done with a lot of resources," Musk claimed in a post.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: How the Futures Command is helping Army to envision NGC2
item.description: Joe Welch, the deputy to the commanding general of the Army Futures Command, said the characteristics of need helps define the problem set for industry. The post How the Futures Command is helping Army to envision NGC2 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Vibes won’t make your software successful
item.description: I regret to inform you that vibes don’t pay the bills. Specifically, open source vibes. For as long as I’ve followed open source, there has been a tendency for the industry to focus on normative values of open source, i.e.
item.dc:creator: Info World
